title: Stallioneer scan station update
content:
Stallioneer’s intake flow took a practical step toward real shelf work. This pass focused on making the add-book screen behave more like a scan station: fast to operate, forgiving when a lookup misses, and flexible enough to work with either a barcode scanner or a camera.

What changed in this checkpoint:

  • Added a dedicated speed mode so rapid intake can stay focused on scan, select, save, and move straight to the next book without breaking the overall catalog flow.
  • Added manual entry and camera scan support alongside the existing keyboard-style scanner field, with camera detection using the browser’s native barcode tools when available and a lightweight fallback when it is not.
  • Kept recent scans, sticky defaults, and tag suggestions in the workflow so repetitive entry work takes fewer repeated clicks while the standard mode stays lighter and easier to verify.

This keeps Stallioneer moving toward high-volume real-world cataloging without overcomplicating the base add-book screen.
